name: Vegetable Gumbo
recipeIngredient: 2 cups corn; fresh, frozen, or canned
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large heavy stew pot, combine the bouillon and 1/2 cup white grape juice.
2. Add the onion, green pepper, celery, and garlic. Cook until the vegetables are tender, about 5-7 minutes.
3. Stir in the remaining ingredients: okra, tomatoes, corn, water, tabasco sauce, paprika, parsley, basil or rosemary, and vegetable coating spray.
4. Cook over low heat, stirring occasionally to prevent sticking to the bottom of the pot.
5. Cover and simmer gently until the corn and okra are tender (about 1-2 hours) or cook in a crockpot for 6-7 hours.
Note: To prepare fresh corn, cut it from the cob using a sharp knife and scrape off any remaining kernels. About four ears of corn will yield two cups when prepared this way. If using dried herbs, gently rub them between your palms before adding to ensure their aroma is released.
